message:
chore(beads): sync issue ledger for recent libc ports

Routine beads sync after a batch of recently-shipped feature commits.
No source code changes; the ledger now reflects what already landed in
git history.

Status changes:
- bd-h3pp8 (posix_close): open -> closed as duplicate of bd-zuy8
  (already shipped at 48263c3c with 3 integration tests covering
  valid-fd close, EINVAL on unknown flags, EBADF on invalid fd).

New closed beads recording shipped ports:
- bd-nx5jz: timingsafe_bcmp + timingsafe_memcmp (BSD constant-time
  compare) -> 0b528929; 23 core + 10 abi tests, clippy-clean.
- bd-pjego: strmode (BSD mode-bit-to-ls-style-string) -> ae225490;
  new core::stat module; 28 + 8 tests, clippy-clean.
- bd-vcma4: warnc + vwarnc + errc + verrc (BSD err.h explicit-code
  variants) -> 39d02be1; shared write_err_message_with_code helper;
  12 integration tests, clippy-clean.
- bd-voxzi: strnstr (BSD bounded substring search) -> 7e92d96c;
  bound-respecting via core_strnlen; 12 + 10 tests, clippy-clean.
- bd-xl4sb: freezero + recallocarray (OpenBSD secret-zeroing
  allocators) -> e41feee4; delegates to malloc_abi + explicit_bzero;
  9 integration tests, clippy-clean.

New open bead (now-resolved by 450d6f4c, follow-up to close):
- bd-d8us8: __b64_ntop + __b64_pton (RFC 4648 base64 from
  BIND/libresolv). The shipping commit (450d6f4c) was made
  concurrently by cod-frankenlibc; bead created beforehand will be
  closed in a follow-up sync.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

+{"id":"bd-d8us8","title":"PORT: __b64_ntop + __b64_pton (RFC 4648 base64 from BIND/libresolv)","description":"BIND/libresolv exposes __b64_ntop(src, srclength, target, targsize) and __b64_pton(src, target, targsize) — RFC 4648 base64 encode/decode used by name servers, DNSSEC tools, and programs that link against libresolv. Distinct from existing System V (a64l/l64a) and crypt(3) base64 modules: standard MIME alphabet (A-Z a-z 0-9 + /) with = padding, big-endian bit packing. Currently missing entirely from frankenlibc-abi (no b64_ntop/b64_pton/__b64_ntop/__b64_pton symbols). Ship a new core::resolv::b64 module (encode/decode pure-byte) plus 4 abi shims (b64_ntop, b64_pton, and the leading-underscore aliases __b64_ntop/__b64_pton that libresolv actually exports).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-25T15:22:29.270258169Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T15:28:18.197693080Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T15:28:18.195790239Z","close_reason":"shipped: core::resolv::b64 (RFC 4648 ntop/pton) + glibc_internal_abi b64_ntop/b64_pton/__b64_ntop/__b64_pton; 17+12 tests; clippy-clean","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-gwqja","title":"[MODE-4][MEDIUM] resource bounds: host thread trampoline spins unbounded","description":"[MODE-4][MEDIUM] resource bounds: host thread start trampoline spins without a bound while waiting for handoff

The host-thread bootstrap path contains an unbounded busy-wait:
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:1390-1399` loops on `start_ctx.host_thread.load(Ordering::Acquire)` until the parent stores a non-zero thread id.
- the parent only performs that store after `host_create` returns and after writing the thread id back to the caller (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:1231-1252` and `1356-1380`).

Under normal scheduling this window is short, but there is no bound, no backoff, and no fallback park. If the creator thread is delayed under load, preempted in a bad spot, or stuck before the store, the child burns CPU in a pure spin loop.

That is a resource-bounds risk rather than a functional mismatch: it can turn thread creation pressure into avoidable CPU amplification.

Fix direction:
- replace the open-ended spin with a bounded handoff protocol, or
- initialize the host thread identity in a way that does not require the child to busy-wait on parent-side publication.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:46:28.884118055Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:42:20.278149696Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:42:20.276052826Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["modes","pthread","resource-bounds","review"]}

 {"id":"bd-h5x","title":"EPIC: Remove glibc callthrough (raw syscalls + pthread primitives)","description":"Critique mapping: #2.

Goal: eliminate dependency on system glibc for core I/O/threading primitives when we claim replacement.

Deliverables:
- Raw syscall layer for Linux x86_64 (no libc::syscall).
- Futex-based pthread mutex primitives.
- Migrate ABIs to use the raw layer.

Acceptance:
- rg finds no libc pthread mutex call-through in the ‘replacement’ build profile.
- E2E smoke passes.

Verification Mandate:
- Every implementation child bead MUST ship comprehensive unit tests and deterministic e2e scripts (strict + hardened where applicable).
- Every test/e2e execution MUST emit detailed structured logs (trace_id, mode, symbol/API family, decision path, errno/outcome, timing, and artifact pointers).
- No bead may close without: test commands, expected outputs, and failure-log artifact examples documented in bead notes or linked reports.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.

## Success Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Success Criteria

### Mandatory Verification Gate
1. Unit Test Evidence
- Add deterministic unit tests for nominal, boundary, and invalid-input paths in the touched modules.
- Include strict and hardened mode assertions when behavior differs by runtime mode.
- Include failure-mode tests covering repair/deny/default behavior with explicit expected outputs.

2. Integration + E2E Evidence
- Add or extend fixture-driven integration coverage in `tests/conformance` and/or `tests/integration` for this bead scope.
- Add a deterministic E2E script path (or harness command) with explicit setup/teardown and replayable seeds.
- Compare FrankenLibC outputs against host glibc fixtures (or documented replacement-level contract) for all touched symbols.

3. Logging + Traceability Evidence
- Emit structured logs (JSONL or deterministic text) that include bead ID, scenario ID, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, and outcome.
- Verify logs expose repair/deny counters and reason codes so regressions are diagnosable from artifacts alone.
- Record artifact paths in bead notes (test logs, fixture diffs, benchmark outputs, conformance reports).

4. Performance + Regression Evidence
- Capture before/after baseline for latency/throughput on touched hot paths.
- Enforce no-regression threshold or document bounded tradeoff with quantitative rationale.

5. CI Gate Evidence
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo check --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --all-targets`
- all bead-specific harness and E2E scripts pass with deterministic logs archived.","notes":"RusticWolf session 27 (2026-03-09): Nativized 3 GCT symbols (175 remaining, down from 178):

 {"id":"bd-h5x.1","title":"Call-through census + per-level forbidden policy map","description":"Background:

3) Gap report mapped to implementation owners.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Census is reproducible and covers all exported ABI entrypoints.
- Every remaining call-through is classified as permitted temporary exception or blocker.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for census parser and classification logic.
- E2E run on smoke corpus verifying census stability.
- Logs: trace_id, symbol, family, callthrough_target, profile, policy_class.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

### Mandatory Verification Gate
1. Unit Test Evidence
- Add deterministic unit tests for nominal, boundary, and invalid-input paths in the touched modules.
- Include strict and hardened mode assertions when behavior differs by runtime mode.
- Include failure-mode tests covering repair/deny/default behavior with explicit expected outputs.

2. Integration + E2E Evidence
- Add or extend fixture-driven integration coverage in `tests/conformance` and/or `tests/integration` for this bead scope.
- Add a deterministic E2E script path (or harness command) with explicit setup/teardown and replayable seeds.
- Compare FrankenLibC outputs against host glibc fixtures (or documented replacement-level contract) for all touched symbols.

3. Logging + Traceability Evidence
- Emit structured logs (JSONL or deterministic text) that include bead ID, scenario ID, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, and outcome.
- Verify logs expose repair/deny counters and reason codes so regressions are diagnosable from artifacts alone.
- Record artifact paths in bead notes (test logs, fixture diffs, benchmark outputs, conformance reports).

4. Performance + Regression Evidence
- Capture before/after baseline for latency/throughput on touched hot paths.
- Enforce no-regression threshold or document bounded tradeoff with quantitative rationale.

5. CI Gate Evidence
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo check --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --all-targets`
- all bead-specific harness and E2E scripts pass with deterministic logs archived.","notes":"## Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist

 {"id":"bd-vj3","title":"Perf: Per-kernel microbench suite (criterion groups + thresholds)","description":"Add a criterion microbench suite that measures *per-kernel* costs, not just end-to-end decide().

End-to-end `decide()` can hide regressions. Per-kernel benches let us pin blame when a new kernel or refactor shifts cost.

## Bench Design
- Criterion group per kernel (or per family of kernels).
- Measure:
  - `decide` contributions (hot modules)
  - cadence update costs (epoch modules)
- Report ns/op and p95/p99 where applicable.

## Dependency
- Must reuse the baseline harness from `bd-d5l` and regression thresholds from `bd-242`.

- A new kernel cannot land without adding/adjusting its microbench.

 {"id":"bd-vq1g","title":"D.1: (SUPERSEDED) — folded into parent bd-9dq5","description":"## Goal
Implement the request-tracking infrastructure for async DNS and the gai_error/gai_cancel functions.

## Design

### Request Registry
A global mutex-protected HashMap<usize, AsyncDnsRequest> maps gaicb pointer addresses to request state. The gaicb struct (defined by POSIX) contains:
- ar_name: hostname to resolve
- ar_service: service name/port
- ar_request: hints (addrinfo struct)
- ar_result: output addrinfo list (written on completion)

### gai_error(req: *mut gaicb) -> c_int
1. Look up req address in registry
2. If not found: return EAI_SYSTEM (unknown request)
3. Return state: EAI_INPROGRESS (still running), 0 (completed successfully), EAI_CANCELLED, or the error code from getaddrinfo

### gai_cancel(req: *mut gaicb) -> c_int
1. Look up req in registry
2. If not found: return EAI_NOCANCEL
3. If already completed: return EAI_ALLDONE
4. If in progress: set CANCELLED flag, return EAI_CANCELLED (or EAI_NOTCANCELED if thread already started resolution)

### State Constants
- EAI_INPROGRESS = -100 (glibc value)
- EAI_CANCELLED = -101
- EAI_ALLDONE = -103
- EAI_NOTCANCELED = -102

## Implementation
File: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs (near existing gai_* functions)
- Add AsyncDnsRequest struct
- Add static ASYNC_DNS_REGISTRY: LazyLock<Mutex<HashMap<usize, AsyncDnsRequest>>>
- Replace gai_error body
- Replace gai_cancel body

## Testing
- gai_error on unknown request returns EAI_SYSTEM
- gai_cancel on unknown request returns EAI_NOCANCEL
- Register a fake completed request, gai_error returns 0
- Register a fake in-progress request, gai_cancel returns EAI_CANCELLED

## Acceptance Criteria
State machine is correct. Registry tracks requests. Tests pass.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:19:44.987634072Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T01:59:38.338086567Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T01:59:38.337881268Z","close_reason":"Superseded: Track D redesigned as synchronous-only.  {"id":"bd-xpgak","title":"xdr_pointer / xdr_reference: no depth limit on caller-supplied recursive procs","description":"## Defect

`xdr_pointer` (rpc_abi.rs:1399) and `xdr_reference` (rpc_abi.rs:1362)
take a caller-supplied per-element xdr_proc. If the caller passes a
proc that recursively invokes xdr_pointer on a nested field (e.g.,
a generated stub for an XDR type with a self-referential
optional pointer like `struct tree { tree *left; tree *right;}`),
attacker-supplied wire data of repeated `more=1` flags walks the
recursion arbitrarily deep — same DoS class as bd-lc5ss but
caller-controlled rather than library-internal.

bd-lc5ss fixed the library's own internal recursive type
(xdr_pmaplist) by rewriting iteratively. That fix doesn't help
caller-supplied recursive types; the library has no per-thread
depth tracker on xdr_pointer/xdr_reference at all.

## Fix

Add a `XDR_RECURSE_DEPTH` thread_local Cell<usize> with a hard cap
(e.g. 256). xdr_reference increments on entry, decrements on exit,
and returns XDR_FALSE if the counter exceeds the cap. This is
defense-in-depth: callers using rpcgen-generated stubs with
sensible types never approach the cap, but a malicious wire stream
fed to a vulnerable caller is bounded by the depth limit instead
of by available stack.

## Severity

[MEDIUM] (P3). The library-internal recursive type was fixed by
bd-lc5ss; this remaining attack surface requires a vulnerable
CALLER (one that passes a recursive proc), so it's a smaller blast
radius than bd-lc5ss but still a real wire-controlled DoS for any
RPC server that exposes recursive XDR types.","notes":"Fixed in 9d326b98: XDR_REFERENCE_DEPTH thread_local Cell<u32> with hard cap of 256. xdr_reference increments on entry, decrements on exit. Added regression test xdr_reference_depth_limit_refuses_deep_recursive_wire_without_overflow with 1024-deep recursive RecursiveNode chain. 90/90 xdr_abi_test pass; clippy clean.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T02:00:51.865514841Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T03:53:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T03:53:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
